assembly_commands.name: ledger-empty-root
assembly_commands.meaning: Merkle Tree Hash for an empty WUCI-LEDGER

assembly_commands.name: ledger-leaf-file
assembly_commands.meaning: Domain-separated WUCI-LEDGER leaf hash for exact entry file bytes

assembly_commands.name: ledger-node
assembly_commands.input: left and right are 64-hex SHA-256 child commitments

zig_commands.name: init
zig_commands.meaning: Initialize an empty append-only WUCI-LEDGER with tree-size 0

zig_commands.name: append
zig_commands.meaning: Verify a public WUCI-WITNESS bundle, derive a canonical ledger entry, append it, and write the next ledger head

zig_commands.name: prove-inclusion
zig_commands.meaning: Emit a Merkle inclusion proof for a sequence under the current ledger head

zig_commands.name: verify-inclusion
zig_commands.meaning: Verify that a canonical ledger entry is included under a ledger head

zig_commands.name: prove-consistency
zig_commands.meaning: Emit an append-only consistency proof between two ledger heads

zig_commands.name: verify-consistency
zig_commands.meaning: Verify that a later ledger head extends an earlier ledger head

zig_commands.name: verify-history
zig_commands.meaning: Verify every entry, every numbered head, previous-head links, latest pointers, and local append-only history

python_reference_commands: tools/wuci_ledger.py remains a regression/reference harness for the same fixed formats.
format_constraints: Ledger entries are fixed-order label/value lines.
Leaf hashing uses exact entry file bytes, including the final newline.
Internal node inputs are decoded 32-byte hashes, not ASCII hex bytes.
Assembly owns the hash commitment primitives.
Zig owns the active append, inclusion proof, consistency proof, and verify-history orchestration lane.
Ledger append must verify the public WUCI-WITNESS bundle before deriving a leaf.
Inclusion and consistency proof hashing must preserve the assembly ledger primitive domain separation.
non_goals: Do not parse publish attestations in assembly in this first ledger step.
Do not accept arbitrary signer material.
Do not replace WUCI-PUBLISH or WUCI-WITNESS verification.
Do not add new secret-bearing crypto.
